claim_bullets.bullet_text: Functions are reference types in JavaScript (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.31-31).
Each evaluation of a function expression produces a new function (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.31-31).
Parentheses are used to appease the JavaScript parser when defining functions (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.31-31).

source_record_text: A section on functions and identities in JavaScript Allongé, discussing how functions are reference types and how evaluating expressions produces new, non-identical functions.
